learning_outcomes.description: Explain key concepts and issues in global health and global change, including the influence of environmental, social and economic determinants of infectious diseases.
Apply epidemiological principles to describe and interpret the global burden and distribution of major infectious diseases, particularly in low- and middle-income countries.
Analyse patterns, determinants and transmission dynamics of infectious diseases to understand their impact across different populations and settings.
Critically evaluate global and local strategies, policies, and evidence used to prevent and control infectious diseases, identifying strengths, limitations, and areas for improvement.

Develop and communicate evidence-based strategies for the prevention and control of infectious diseases, demonstrating effective written, oral, and collaborative skills, including teamwork and reflective practice.

assessments.task_description:
Task Description: Reflection on Global Health and Infectious Diseases

Overview: You are required to write an individual reflection on global health and infectious diseases. Your reflection will be based on your lectures, tutorials, and workshops on global change, global health, global health security, and infectious diseases. You are expected to:

· Reflect on five key lessons you learned about global change, global health, global health security. and infectious diseases.

· Provide three initiatives you will advocate for global change, global health, global health security, and infectious diseases; critically justify why you advocate for them.

Writing and Submission Guidelines

· Language and Style: Use first-person language with a focus on your personal experience in the team-based assessment.

· Reflection: Take the time to thoroughly reflect on your experiences and question your assumptions, beliefs, and viewpoints.

· Structure: Organize your reflections clearly, addressing all the specified points.

· Length: The reflection should be no more than 800 words.

Submission: Submit your reflections via Turnitin submission link.

These reflections are not just academic exercises but are intended to deepen your understanding of global health issues and improve your ability to contribute to health security.
assessments.submission_guidelines: It should be submitted via the Turnitin submission link.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
8/09/2026 - 25/09/2026

The oral examinations will be conducted over a three-week period, from 8 September 2026 to 25 September 2026. Students will be scheduled for the examination in alphabetical order.
assessments.task_description:
Oral Examination (Weeks 1–7)

The oral examination assesses students’ understanding and application of concepts covered in Weeks 1–7, including global health, global change, determinants of health, infectious disease epidemiology, global health security, and major blood-borne, air-borne, and vector-borne diseases. Students will respond to questions requiring them to explain key concepts, interpret epidemiological information, analyze disease patterns and determinants, and evaluate prevention and control strategies, particularly in low- and middle-income countries. The examination evaluates knowledge, critical thinking, application of epidemiological principles, and the ability to communicate evidence-based responses clearly and professionally in a discussion format.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

The list of groups presenting in each round will be provided via Blackboard.
assessments.task_description:
This is a group assignment.

You are members of the Communicable Disease Unit within the Ministry of Health of one of the countries allocated below. Your unit has been commissioned to undertake a burden of disease analysis to identify a communicable disease of major public health significance and assess its impact on population health and development within the country.

Based on available epidemiological and health data, your group will select one infectious disease of significant public health importance in the assigned country. You will then analyze the disease burden, examine factors influencing its distribution and impact, evaluate current prevention and control efforts, and assess progress toward relevant Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) targets.

Your group will prepare a presentation that:

  1. Conducts a burden of disease analysis to identify and justify the selection of a public health–significant infectious disease in the assigned country.
  2. Describes and analyses the epidemiology of the selected infectious disease, including its incidence, prevalence, mortality, morbidity, distribution, and trends over time.
  3. Examines the socioeconomic impact of the disease on individuals, communities, the healthcare system, and national development.
  4. Identifies and analyzes the determinants contributing to the burden and transmission of the disease, including environmental, social, economic, demographic, behavioral, and health-system factors.
  5. Evaluates current policies, programs, and interventions used to prevent and control the disease, highlighting key successes and ongoing challenges.
  6. Assesses progress towards relevant SDG targets and indicators associated with the disease and broader health outcomes within the country.
  7. Develops evidence-based recommendations for strengthening disease prevention, control, and management strategies to reduce the burden of disease and support achievement of the SDG targets.

The presentation should demonstrate the use of credible epidemiological data, relevant scientific literature, and national and international health reports to support analysis and recommendations.

List of Groups (Countries):

  • Brazil
  • Canada
  • China
  • Democratic Republic of Congo
  • England
  • Germany
  • India
  • Indonesia
  • Soudi Arabia
  • South Africa

assessments.task_description:
You are a research officer working for the Department of Health of a country of your choice. You are expected to outline the strategies towards controlling, ending, eliminating, or eradicating a health problem.

Task Description: You will choose an infectious disease of global health importance, conduct a literature search, and identify ten best articles to review the literature on the epidemiology and management of the disease in a particular country. It is important that the health problem is related to the Sustainable Development Goals. These diseases can be: HIV/AIDS, TB, Malaria, Hepatitis, Neglected tropical diseases, water- and food-born diseases, Zoonotic diseases, and others which are discussed during the course. 

You will be assessed for your ability to provide a convincing argument using the following outline:

  • Background: Identify the global health problem and describe its epidemiology and the response to it. Describe the plan/strategy towards controlling, ending, eliminating, or eradicating the health problem. State the objectives of your review.
  • Methods: Describe the method you are using, the databases you utilized, the search words, and the selection of the ten best resources.
  • Findings: Present the main findings of the ten resources one-by-one focusing on the epidemiology of the health problem, response to the health problem (including successes and challenges), and strategies toward controlling, ending, eliminating, or eradicating the health problem. You need to present findings in a way that ensures your review objectives are achieved.
  • Discussion: Provide a synthesis (summary) of the findings from the ten resources, a conclusion, and a recommendation.

Referencing: Vancouver (Please search for guides in the Library). 

Academic Integrity: Check the Academic Integrity section in the Library for a good understanding of how to use references as all Papers will be checked by Turnitin. A practice link into Turnitin has been set up for you in the Learning Resources section in this site so you can check your own originality report before you submit it. Further instructions are there for you to use this for practice. 

Formatting: Use Arial, a minimum font size of 11, 1.5 or double-spaced, and paginated throughout, including references and tables, with margins of at least 2.5 cm. The text should be left justified. Save as a PDF for submission.

How will this be marked? Check the marking criteria posted in the BB.

Audience: As per the presentations, you are preparing the article review as a report for the Health Minister of your chosen country.

First person: No first person. It should show an expert opinion in the third person

Article Preparation: (See sample Paper)

Title Page: Author's name; Title; 3-5 Key Messages, detailing concisely the main points made in the paper; A word count of the full article - 2,500 words; Title Page and References are not included in the word count.

Abbreviations: Non-standard abbreviations should be defined at the first occurrence and introduced only where multiple use is made. Do not use abbreviations in headings.

NOTE: There is a task sheet attached which will give you details and how to get started. Exemplars will be shown to students during the semester break in good time for you to check your work.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The report should be submitted through the Turnitin submission link

learning_activities.topic:
Introduction to Global Health and Infectious Diseases: Lecture

This lecture introduces the concepts of global health and infectious diseases, highlighting their significance in improving population health and achieving health equity worldwide. Students will explore the burden, determinants, transmission, prevention, and control of major infectious diseases affecting global populations. The lecture will also provide an overview of the course structure, including key topics to be covered over the 13-week semester. In addition, students will be introduced to the learning outcomes, teaching and learning activities, and assessment tasks that will support their knowledge development, critical thinking, and application of global health principles throughout the course.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_title: Introduction to Global Health and Infectious Diseases: Lecture
learning_activities.topic_description: Introduction to Global Health and Infectious Diseases: Lecture This lecture introduces the concepts of global health and infectious diseases, highlighting their significance in improving population health and achieving health equity worldwide. Students will explore the burden, determinants, transmission, prevention, and control of major infectious diseases affecting global populations. The lecture will also provide an overview of the course structure, including key topics to be covered over the 13-week semester. In addition, students will be introduced to the learning outcomes, teaching and learning activities, and assessment tasks that will support their knowledge development, critical thinking, and application of global health principles throughout the course.

learning_activities.topic:
Global Change, Global Health and Infectious Diseases: Tutorial and Workshop

This tutorial and workshop provides an opportunity for students to ask questions about the course content, learning activities, assessment tasks, and expectations for successful participation throughout the semester. Students will be introduced to group enrollment procedures, collaborative learning requirements, and the purpose of the team charter. Through guided activities, groups will establish expectations, roles, communication strategies, and responsibilities to support effective teamwork and preparation for the group assignment and other course assessments.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_title: Global Change, Global Health and Infectious Diseases: Tutorial and Workshop
learning_activities.topic_description: Global Change, Global Health and Infectious Diseases: Tutorial and Workshop This tutorial and workshop provides an opportunity for students to ask questions about the course content, learning activities, assessment tasks, and expectations for successful participation throughout the semester. Students will be introduced to group enrollment procedures, collaborative learning requirements, and the purpose of the team charter. Through guided activities, groups will establish expectations, roles, communication strategies, and responsibilities to support effective teamwork and preparation for the group assignment and other course assessments.

learning_activities.topic:
Global Change: Lecture

This lecture examines major global changes that influence population health and the emergence and spread of infectious diseases. Key topics include climate change, migration, urbanization, technological advancements, demographic transitions, globalization, and environmental changes. Students will explore how these factors shape disease patterns, health risks, healthcare access, and health inequalities across different regions of the world. The lecture will also discuss the interconnected nature of global health challenges and the need for multidisciplinary approaches to address them. Understanding these global drivers is essential for developing effective public health policies and interventions in a rapidly changing world.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_title: Global Change: Lecture

The lecture will also discuss the interconnected nature of global health challenges and the need for multidisciplinary approaches to address them. Understanding these global drivers is essential for developing effective public health policies and interventions in a rapidly changing world.

learning_activities.topic:
Global Change, Global Health and Infectious Diseases: Tutorial and Workshop

This tutorial and workshop explores how global changes, including climate change, migration, urbanization, technological advances, and demographic shifts, influence global health and infectious diseases. Through discussions, case studies, and group activities, students will analyze real-world challenges and examine public health strategies for disease prevention, control, and health promotion.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_title: Global Change, Global Health and Infectious Diseases: Tutorial and Workshop
learning_activities.topic_description: Global Change, Global Health and Infectious Diseases: Tutorial and Workshop This tutorial and workshop explores how global changes, including climate change, migration, urbanization, technological advances, and demographic shifts, influence global health and infectious diseases. Through discussions, case studies, and group activities, students will analyze real-world challenges and examine public health strategies for disease prevention, control, and health promotion.

learning_activities.topic:
Global Health: Sustainable Development Goals: Lecture

This lecture traces the evolution of global health from the Primary Health Care movement established through the Alma-Ata Declaration in 1978 to the current Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). Students will examine key milestones, including the Millennium Development Goals (MDGs), major global health initiatives, and shifting approaches to health equity, disease prevention, and health system strengthening. The lecture highlights how global health priorities have evolved over time and discusses the role of international organizations, governments, and communities in addressing contemporary global health challenges.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_title: Global Health: Sustainable Development Goals: Lecture
learning_activities.topic_description: Global Health: Sustainable Development Goals: Lecture This lecture traces the evolution of global health from the Primary Health Care movement established through the Alma-Ata Declaration in 1978 to the current Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). Students will examine key milestones, including the Millennium Development Goals (MDGs), major global health initiatives, and shifting approaches to health equity, disease prevention, and health system strengthening. The lecture highlights how global health priorities have evolved over time and discusses the role of international organizations, governments, and communities in addressing contemporary global health challenges.

learning_activities.topic:
Global Health and Infectious Diseases: Tutorial and Workshop

This tutorial and workshop will engage students in discussions and activities exploring the historical development of global health. Through case studies and group exercises, students will examine key global health milestones, their impact on infectious disease control, and ongoing challenges in achieving equitable health outcomes worldwide. They will also conduct a burden of disease analysis to identify and justify a priority infectious disease of public health significance in the assigned country.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_title: Global Health and Infectious Diseases: Tutorial and Workshop
learning_activities.topic_description: Global Health and Infectious Diseases: Tutorial and Workshop This tutorial and workshop will engage students in discussions and activities exploring the historical development of global health. Through case studies and group exercises, students will examine key global health milestones, their impact on infectious disease control, and ongoing challenges in achieving equitable health outcomes worldwide. They will also conduct a burden of disease analysis to identify and justify a priority infectious disease of public health significance in the assigned country.

learning_activities.topic:
Global Health Security: Lecture

This lecture examines key global frameworks designed to strengthen preparedness, detection, and response to public health threats. Students will explore the Global Health Security Agenda (GHSA), the International Health Regulations (IHR), and the Pandemic Accord, focusing on their roles in enhancing international cooperation, health system capacity, surveillance, and emergency response. The lecture will discuss lessons learned from recent disease outbreaks and pandemics, as well as ongoing challenges and opportunities in achieving global health security and preventing future infectious disease threats.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_title: Global Health Security: Lecture

Students will explore the Global Health Security Agenda (GHSA), the International Health Regulations (IHR), and the Pandemic Accord, focusing on their roles in enhancing international cooperation, health system capacity, surveillance, and emergency response. The lecture will discuss lessons learned from recent disease outbreaks and pandemics, as well as ongoing challenges and opportunities in achieving global health security and preventing future infectious disease threats.

learning_activities.topic:
Global Health and Infectious Diseases: Tutorial and Workshop

This tutorial and workshop will use a case study to apply concepts from the Global Health Security Agenda, International Health Regulations, and the Pandemic Accord. Students will work collaboratively to describe and analyze the epidemiological profile of a selected infectious disease.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_title: Global Health and Infectious Diseases: Tutorial and Workshop

Students will work collaboratively to describe and analyze the epidemiological profile of a selected infectious disease.

learning_activities.topic:
Epidemiology and Management of Blood-borne Diseases: Lecture

This lecture introduces the epidemiology, transmission, prevention, and management of major blood-borne diseases, including HIV, hepatitis B, and hepatitis C. Students will examine the global burden of these infections, risk factors, disease distribution, and vulnerable populations. The lecture will also explore screening, diagnosis, treatment, prevention strategies, infection control measures, and public health interventions aimed at reducing transmission and improving health outcomes at individual and population levels.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_title: Epidemiology and Management of Blood-borne Diseases: Lecture
learning_activities.topic_description: Epidemiology and Management of Blood-borne Diseases: Lecture This lecture introduces the epidemiology, transmission, prevention, and management of major blood-borne diseases, including HIV, hepatitis B, and hepatitis C. Students will examine the global burden of these infections, risk factors, disease distribution, and vulnerable populations. The lecture will also explore screening, diagnosis, treatment, prevention strategies, infection control measures, and public health interventions aimed at reducing transmission and improving health outcomes at individual and population levels.

learning_activities.topic:
Global Health and Infectious Diseases: Tutorial and Workshop

This workshop will use a case study on a blood-borne disease to explore its epidemiology, transmission, prevention, and management. Through guided discussion and group activities, students will examine the broader social, economic, and public health consequences of the disease, including its impact on healthcare systems, productivity, health inequities, and national development. Students will critically analyze evidence and discuss strategies to reduce disease burden and improve health outcomes.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_title: Global Health and Infectious Diseases: Tutorial and Workshop
learning_activities.topic_description: Global Health and Infectious Diseases: Tutorial and Workshop This workshop will use a case study on a blood-borne disease to explore its epidemiology, transmission, prevention, and management. Through guided discussion and group activities, students will examine the broader social, economic, and public health consequences of the disease, including its impact on healthcare systems, productivity, health inequities, and national development. Students will critically analyze evidence and discuss strategies to reduce disease burden and improve health outcomes.

learning_activities.topic:
Epidemiology and Management of Air-borne Diseases: Lecture

This lecture examines the epidemiology, transmission, prevention, and control of major air-borne diseases, including tuberculosis (TB), COVID-19, and influenza. Students will explore the global burden of these diseases, risk factors, patterns of transmission, and their impact on public health. The lecture will also discuss surveillance, vaccination, infection prevention and control measures, treatment strategies, and the role of public health systems in managing outbreaks and reducing disease transmission at local, national, and global levels.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_title: Epidemiology and Management of Air-borne Diseases: Lecture
learning_activities.topic_description: Epidemiology and Management of Air-borne Diseases: Lecture This lecture examines the epidemiology, transmission, prevention, and control of major air-borne diseases, including tuberculosis (TB), COVID-19, and influenza. Students will explore the global burden of these diseases, risk factors, patterns of transmission, and their impact on public health. The lecture will also discuss surveillance, vaccination, infection prevention and control measures, treatment strategies, and the role of public health systems in managing outbreaks and reducing disease transmission at local, national, and global levels.

learning_activities.topic:
Global Health and Infectious Diseases: Tutorial and Workshop

This workshop will use a case study on an air-borne disease, such as tuberculosis, COVID-19, or influenza, to examine its epidemiology, transmission dynamics, prevention, and control strategies. Through group discussions and data analysis, students will explore the challenges associated with outbreak management and public health responses in different settings. In this tutorial, students will identify and analyze the biological, environmental, social, economic, and behavioral factors contributing to the burden and transmission of airborne diseases. Through interactive discussions and problem-solving activities, students will evaluate how these factors influence disease spread and assess evidence-based interventions to reduce transmission and improve population health outcomes.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_title: Global Health and Infectious Diseases: Tutorial and Workshop
learning_activities.topic_description: Global Health and Infectious Diseases: Tutorial and Workshop This workshop will use a case study on an air-borne disease, such as tuberculosis, COVID-19, or influenza, to examine its epidemiology, transmission dynamics, prevention, and control strategies. Through group discussions and data analysis, students will explore the challenges associated with outbreak management and public health responses in different settings. In this tutorial, students will identify and analyze the biological, environmental, social, economic, and behavioral factors contributing to the burden and transmission of airborne diseases. Through interactive discussions and problem-solving activities, students will evaluate how these factors influence disease spread and assess evidence-based interventions to reduce transmission and improve population health outcomes.

learning_activities.topic:
Epidemiology and Management of Vector-borne Diseases: Lecture

This lecture examines the epidemiology, transmission, prevention, and control of major vector-borne diseases, including malaria, yellow fever, Zika virus, dengue, and chikungunya. Students will explore the role of vectors such as mosquitoes and ticks in disease transmission, the global burden and distribution of vector-borne diseases, and the influence of environmental and climatic factors on their spread. The lecture will also discuss surveillance, vaccination, vector control strategies, outbreak response, and public health interventions aimed at reducing disease transmission and improving population health outcomes.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_title: Epidemiology and Management of Vector-borne Diseases: Lecture
learning_activities.topic_description: Epidemiology and Management of Vector-borne Diseases: Lecture This lecture examines the epidemiology, transmission, prevention, and control of major vector-borne diseases, including malaria, yellow fever, Zika virus, dengue, and chikungunya. Students will explore the role of vectors such as mosquitoes and ticks in disease transmission, the global burden and distribution of vector-borne diseases, and the influence of environmental and climatic factors on their spread. The lecture will also discuss surveillance, vaccination, vector control strategies, outbreak response, and public health interventions aimed at reducing disease transmission and improving population health outcomes.

learning_activities.topic:
Global Health and Infectious Diseases: Tutorial and Workshop

The workshop will use a case study on a vector-borne disease, such as malaria, yellow fever, Zika virus, or dengue, to explore its epidemiology, transmission patterns, risk factors, and public health impact. Through group discussions and data analysis, students will examine disease outbreaks and evaluate challenges in prevention and control.

Students will also critically evaluate existing strategies, policies, and interventions used to prevent and control infectious diseases. Students will assess the effectiveness, strengths, and limitations of these approaches and propose evidence-based recommendations for improving disease prevention and control.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05

Through group discussions and data analysis, students will examine disease outbreaks and evaluate challenges in prevention and control. Students will also critically evaluate existing strategies, policies, and interventions used to prevent and control infectious diseases. Students will assess the effectiveness, strengths, and limitations of these approaches and propose evidence-based recommendations for improving disease prevention and control.

learning_activities.topic:
Epidemiology and Management of Water- and Food-borne Diseases: Lecture

This lecture examines the epidemiology, transmission, prevention, and management of major water- and food-borne diseases, including Salmonella, Cholera, Shigella, Escherichia coli (E. coli), and other enteric infections. Students will explore the global burden and distribution of these diseases, risk factors, sources of contamination, and pathways of transmission. The lecture will discuss disease surveillance, outbreak investigation, water, sanitation and hygiene (WASH) interventions, food safety measures, clinical management, and public health strategies aimed at preventing and controlling water- and food-borne diseases and reducing their impact on population health.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_title: Epidemiology and Management of Water- and Food-borne Diseases: Lecture
learning_activities.topic_description: Epidemiology and Management of Water- and Food-borne Diseases: Lecture This lecture examines the epidemiology, transmission, prevention, and management of major water- and food-borne diseases, including Salmonella, Cholera, Shigella, Escherichia coli (E. coli), and other enteric infections. Students will explore the global burden and distribution of these diseases, risk factors, sources of contamination, and pathways of transmission. The lecture will discuss disease surveillance, outbreak investigation, water, sanitation and hygiene (WASH) interventions, food safety measures, clinical management, and public health strategies aimed at preventing and controlling water- and food-borne diseases and reducing their impact on population health.

learning_activities.topic:
Global Health and Infectious Diseases: Tutorial and Workshop

The workshop will use a case study on a water- or food-borne disease, such as cholera, salmonellosis, shigellosis, or E. coli infection, to explore its epidemiology, transmission, risk factors, outbreak investigation, and public health impact. Through group discussions and case analysis, students will examine disease prevention, control measures, and challenges in outbreak management.

Students will assess how a country's response to a selected disease contributes to achieving relevant Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) targets. Students will critically evaluate policies, programs, and interventions, and discuss opportunities to strengthen disease prevention and improve population health outcomes.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05

Through group discussions and case analysis, students will examine disease prevention, control measures, and challenges in outbreak management. Students will assess how a country's response to a selected disease contributes to achieving relevant Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) targets. Students will critically evaluate policies, programs, and interventions, and discuss opportunities to strengthen disease prevention and improve population health outcomes.

learning_activities.topic:
Epidemiology and Management of Neglected Tropical Diseases: Lecture

This lecture examines the epidemiology, transmission, prevention, and management of major neglected tropical diseases (NTDs), including Leishmaniasis, Onchocerciasis (River Blindness), Lymphatic Filariasis, Schistosomiasis, and Chagas disease. Students will explore the global burden and distribution of NTDs, their social and environmental determinants, and their disproportionate impact on vulnerable and underserved populations. The lecture will discuss disease surveillance, diagnosis, treatment, vector control, mass drug administration, and integrated public health approaches aimed at reducing disease burden, improving health equity, and supporting the elimination and control of NTDs in endemic regions.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_title: Epidemiology and Management of Neglected Tropical Diseases: Lecture
learning_activities.topic_description: Epidemiology and Management of Neglected Tropical Diseases: Lecture This lecture examines the epidemiology, transmission, prevention, and management of major neglected tropical diseases (NTDs), including Leishmaniasis, Onchocerciasis (River Blindness), Lymphatic Filariasis, Schistosomiasis, and Chagas disease. Students will explore the global burden and distribution of NTDs, their social and environmental determinants, and their disproportionate impact on vulnerable and underserved populations. The lecture will discuss disease surveillance, diagnosis, treatment, vector control, mass drug administration, and integrated public health approaches aimed at reducing disease burden, improving health equity, and supporting the elimination and control of NTDs in endemic regions.

learning_activities.topic:
Global Health and Infectious Diseases: Tutorial and Workshop

The workshop will use a case study on a neglected tropical disease, such as leishmaniasis, onchocerciasis, or lymphatic filariasis, to explore its epidemiology, transmission, risk factors, health impacts, and control strategies. Through group discussions and case analysis, students will examine the challenges of prevention, diagnosis, treatment, and disease elimination in resource-limited settings.

Students will develop evidence-based recommendations to reduce the burden of selected infectious diseases and accelerate progress toward relevant Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) targets. Students will critically assess existing policies and interventions, identify gaps in current approaches, and propose strategies that promote health equity, strengthen health systems, and improve outcomes for affected populations and communities.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05

Through group discussions and case analysis, students will examine the challenges of prevention, diagnosis, treatment, and disease elimination in resource-limited settings. Students will develop evidence-based recommendations to reduce the burden of selected infectious diseases and accelerate progress toward relevant Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) targets. Students will critically assess existing policies and interventions, identify gaps in current approaches, and propose strategies that promote health equity, strengthen health systems, and improve outcomes for affected populations and communities.

learning_activities.topic:
One Health and Zoonotic Diseases: Lecture

This lecture introduces the One Health approach, emphasizing the interconnectedness of human, animal, and environmental health in the prevention and control of zoonotic diseases. Students will examine the epidemiology, transmission, prevention, and management of key zoonotic diseases, including rabies, anthrax, leptospirosis, and Q fever. The lecture will explore the role of animal reservoirs, environmental factors, and human activities in disease emergence and spread. Students will also discuss surveillance, outbreak response, intersectoral collaboration, and public health strategies aimed at reducing the burden of zoonotic diseases and improving global health security.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_title: One Health and Zoonotic Diseases: Lecture
learning_activities.topic_description: One Health and Zoonotic Diseases: Lecture This lecture introduces the One Health approach, emphasizing the interconnectedness of human, animal, and environmental health in the prevention and control of zoonotic diseases. Students will examine the epidemiology, transmission, prevention, and management of key zoonotic diseases, including rabies, anthrax, leptospirosis, and Q fever. The lecture will explore the role of animal reservoirs, environmental factors, and human activities in disease emergence and spread. Students will also discuss surveillance, outbreak response, intersectoral collaboration, and public health strategies aimed at reducing the burden of zoonotic diseases and improving global health security.

learning_activities.topic:
Global Health and Infectious Diseases: Seminar

The seminar provides an opportunity for student groups to present and discuss their burden of disease analysis for an assigned country. Using epidemiological data and burden of disease indicators, groups will identify and justify the selection of one infectious disease of major public health significance. Students will critically analyze the disease's epidemiology, determinants, transmission patterns, socioeconomic impact, and current prevention and control measures. The seminar will also examine the country's progress towards relevant Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) targets and facilitate the development of evidence-based recommendations to strengthen public health responses, reduce disease burden, and improve population health outcomes.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_title: Global Health and Infectious Diseases: Seminar

The seminar will also examine the country's progress towards relevant Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) targets and facilitate the development of evidence-based recommendations to strengthen public health responses, reduce disease burden, and improve population health outcomes.

learning_activities.topic:
Disasters, Mobility and Infectious Diseases: Lecture

This lecture examines the complex relationships between disasters, human mobility, and infectious diseases in a global health context. Students will explore how natural hazards, climate-related events, conflicts, and humanitarian emergencies contribute to population displacement, migration, and increased vulnerability to infectious diseases. The lecture will discuss the epidemiology of disease outbreaks in disaster and emergency settings, including the factors that influence disease transmission among displaced and affected populations. Students will also examine public health preparedness, surveillance, outbreak response, risk reduction strategies, and the role of national and international agencies in protecting population health during and after disasters.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02

learning_activities.topic:
Antimicrobial Drug Resistance: Lecture

This lecture examines the growing global threat of antimicrobial drug resistance (AMR) and its implications for public health, healthcare systems, and sustainable development. Students will explore the mechanisms by which microorganisms develop resistance to antimicrobial agents, including antibiotics, antivirals, antifungals, and antiparasitic drugs. The lecture will discuss the epidemiology and burden of AMR, key drivers of resistance in human health, animal health, agriculture, and the environment, and the impact of AMR on infectious disease prevention and treatment. Students will also examine surveillance systems, antimicrobial stewardship programs, infection prevention and control measures, the One Health approach, and national and global strategies to combat antimicrobial resistance.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02
